description: Admin Panel — 4-tab admin dashboard (AI Context, Parameters, Monitoring, Users) with full CRUD for system configuration, monitoring backend service with health checks and alerting, and admin user search.

userStories.title: Admin AI Context Endpoints — GET/PATCH
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to view and update the facilitator context bucket and company context bucket so that I can manage AI context for the system.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/admin/ai-context/facilitator returns {id, content, updated_by, updated_at}
PATCH /api/admin/ai-context/facilitator accepts {content} and updates facilitator_context_bucket
GET /api/admin/ai-context/company returns {id, sections, free_text, updated_by, updated_at}
PATCH /api/admin/ai-context/company accepts {sections, free_text} and updates context_agent_bucket
All endpoints require admin role (403 for non-admins)
PATCH endpoints trigger context re-indexing via AI gRPC UpdateContextAgentBucket
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/api-design.md § Admin → AI Context endpoints
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/data-model.md § facilitator_context_bucket, context_agent_bucket
AI: docs/03-ai/agent-architecture.md § Context Agent RAG — re-indexing trigger after company context update
Testing: docs/04-test-architecture/test-matrix.md § T-11.2.01, T-11.2.02 (Integration: AI context CRUD), API-ADMIN.03-06 (API happy path + authz)
Files: services/gateway/apps/admin_ai_context/views.py, services/gateway/apps/admin_ai_context/serializers.py (new), services/gateway/apps/admin_ai_context/tests/test_ai_context.py (new)
Gotchas: FacilitatorContextBucket and ContextAgentBucket are singletons in AI service — use get_or_create pattern. AI service gRPC client already exists (services/gateway/grpc_clients/ai_client.py).

data_model: facilitator_context_bucket: content text, updated_by uuid FK, updated_at timestamptz — singleton table of contents for facilitator AI
context_agent_bucket: sections jsonb, free_text text, updated_by uuid FK, updated_at timestamptz — singleton detailed company info
api_endpoints: GET /api/admin/ai-context/facilitator — returns facilitator bucket → 200 {id, content, updated_by, updated_at}
PATCH /api/admin/ai-context/facilitator {content} — updates facilitator bucket → 200 {id, content, updated_by, updated_at}
GET /api/admin/ai-context/company — returns context agent bucket → 200 {id, sections, free_text, updated_by, updated_at}
PATCH /api/admin/ai-context/company {sections, free_text} — updates context agent bucket → 200 + triggers re-indexing
test_cases: T-11.2.01 | Integration | Facilitator context CRUD | PUT /api/admin/ai-context/facilitator | Content updated
T-11.2.02 | Integration | Context agent bucket CRUD | PUT /api/admin/ai-context/context-agent | Sections + free_text updated
ai_specs: Context Agent RAG — M8 implemented UpdateContextAgentBucket gRPC which re-chunks + re-embeds company context. Trigger this via AI gRPC client after PATCH to context_agent_bucket.

userStories.title: Admin Parameters Endpoints — GET List, PATCH Individual
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to view all system parameters and update individual parameter values so that I can configure runtime behavior without restarting services.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/admin/parameters returns array of all parameters {key, value, default_value, description, data_type, category, updated_by, updated_at}
PATCH /api/admin/parameters/:key accepts {value} and updates admin_parameters table
Validation by data_type: integer/float parsed, boolean 'true'/'false', string no parse
All endpoints require admin role (403 for non-admins)
Changes apply at runtime immediately (no restart required)
Invalid data_type values return 400 with validation error
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/api-design.md § Admin → Parameters endpoints
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/data-model.md § admin_parameters table — runtime-configurable system parameters
Testing: docs/04-test-architecture/test-matrix.md § T-11.3.01 (Integration: parameter update applies immediately), API-ADMIN.01-02, 07-08
Files: services/gateway/apps/admin_config/views.py (replace placeholder), services/gateway/apps/admin_config/serializers.py (new), services/gateway/apps/admin_config/tests/test_parameters.py (new)
Gotchas: AdminParameter table is already seeded with defaults from migration 0002_seed_parameters. Admin parameters are read by various services at runtime via core service (not cached). Data type validation: use serializer field validators for int/float/bool parsing.

data_model: admin_parameters: key varchar(100) PK, value varchar(500), default_value varchar(500), description text, data_type varchar(20) CHECK (string|integer|float|boolean), category varchar(50), updated_by uuid FK, updated_at timestamptz
api_endpoints: GET /api/admin/parameters — list all parameters → 200 [{key, value, default_value, description, data_type, category, updated_by, updated_at}]
PATCH /api/admin/parameters/:key {value} — update parameter → 200 {key, value, ...} | 400 INVALID_VALUE | 404 PARAMETER_NOT_FOUND
test_cases: T-11.3.01 | Integration | Parameter update applies immediately | PATCH /api/admin/parameters/debounce_timer {value: '5'} | Value updated runtime

userStories.title: Admin Monitoring Dashboard Endpoint — GET Aggregate Stats
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to view a monitoring dashboard with active connections, ideas by state, user counts, AI processing stats, and system health so that I can monitor system status.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/admin/monitoring returns {active_connections, ideas_by_state, active_users, online_users, ai_processing, system_health}
active_connections: count of active WebSocket connections (from gateway service)
ideas_by_state: {open, in_review, accepted, dropped, rejected} counts
active_users: total non-deleted users count
online_users: users with WebSocket connections
ai_processing: {request_count, success_count, failure_count} from AI service stats
system_health: {gateway, ai, pdf, notification, database, redis, broker, dlq} with status (healthy/unhealthy) and last_check timestamp
Requires admin role (403 for non-admins)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/api-design.md § Admin → Monitoring endpoints
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/tech-stack.md § Monitoring Service Design — health checks, DLQ depth, AI stats aggregation
Testing: docs/04-test-architecture/test-matrix.md § T-11.4.01 (Integration: dashboard returns data), API-ADMIN.09-10
Files: services/gateway/apps/monitoring/views.py (new), services/gateway/apps/monitoring/serializers.py (new), services/gateway/apps/monitoring/services.py (new), services/gateway/apps/monitoring/tests/test_monitoring.py (new)
Gotchas: Active connections tracked by WebSocket consumer in Redis (check channels layer presence). AI stats available via AI gRPC client. System health is last known status from monitoring service (stored in Redis or DB). DLQ depth queries message broker (RabbitMQ management API or Azure Service Bus SDK).

api_endpoints: GET /api/admin/monitoring — dashboard data → 200 {active_connections, ideas_by_state: {open, in_review, accepted, dropped, rejected}, active_users, online_users, ai_processing: {request_count, success_count, failure_count}, system_health: {gateway, ai, pdf, notification, database, redis, broker, dlq}}
test_cases: T-11.4.01 | Integration | Monitoring dashboard returns data | GET /api/admin/monitoring | All fields populated
API-ADMIN.09 | GET /api/admin/monitoring | Happy path | 200 + stats object
existing_code: services/gateway/websocket/consumers.py: WebSocket consumers track connections in channel groups

userStories.title: Admin Users Search Endpoint — GET with Stats
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to search for users by name or email and view their stats (idea count, review count, contribution count) so that I can find user information quickly.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/admin/users/search?q=query returns filtered user list
Search matches display_name (case-insensitive) OR email (case-insensitive)
Each result includes: {id, email, first_name, last_name, display_name, roles, idea_count, review_count, contribution_count}
idea_count: count of ideas owned (owner_id OR co_owner_id)
review_count: count of review_timeline_entries authored by user
contribution_count: count of chat_messages + board_nodes created by user
Results ordered by display_name alphabetically
Requires admin role (403 for non-admins)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/api-design.md § Admin → Users search endpoint
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/data-model.md § users table — display_name, email, roles. Stats computed from ideas, review_timeline_entries, chat_messages, board_nodes
Testing: docs/04-test-architecture/test-matrix.md § API-ADMIN.11-12
Files: services/gateway/apps/authentication/views.py (add admin_users_search view), services/gateway/apps/authentication/serializers.py (add AdminUserSearchSerializer), services/gateway/apps/authentication/tests/test_admin_users.py (new)
Gotchas: User stats require joins/aggregations across multiple tables. Use Django ORM annotations (Count, Q filters). Search query is optional (empty = return all users). The users table is in gateway service (owned), so direct DB access is fine.

data_model: users: id uuid PK, email varchar UNIQUE, first_name varchar, last_name varchar, display_name varchar, roles varchar[] — user directory
ideas: owner_id uuid FK, co_owner_id uuid FK — count ideas owned
review_timeline_entries: author_id uuid FK — count review actions
chat_messages: sender_id uuid FK, sender_type varchar — count user messages (sender_type='user')
board_nodes: created_by varchar — count nodes where created_by='user' (no user ID link, infer from idea ownership)
api_endpoints: GET /api/admin/users/search?q=query — search users → 200 [{id, email, first_name, last_name, display_name, roles, idea_count, review_count, contribution_count}]
existing_code: services/gateway/apps/authentication/models.py: User model (unmanaged mirror of users table)

userStories.title: Context Re-Indexing Trigger — PATCH Company Context → AI gRPC
userStories.description: As the system, when an admin updates the company context bucket, I want to trigger re-indexing (re-chunk + re-embed) so that the Context Agent RAG has up-to-date information.
acceptanceCriteria: PATCH /api/admin/ai-context/company triggers AI gRPC UpdateContextAgentBucket after successful update
gRPC call passes updated sections and free_text to AI service
AI service deletes existing context_chunks and re-indexes (chunking + embedding)
Error handling: if gRPC fails, return 500 with error details
Re-indexing is async (gRPC returns 202, processing continues in background)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/api-design.md § Admin AI context endpoints — re-indexing on company context update
AI: docs/03-ai/agent-architecture.md § Context Agent RAG pipeline — UpdateContextAgentBucket gRPC implemented in M8
Testing: No dedicated test IDs — integration covered by T-11.2.02 (context agent bucket CRUD)
Files: services/gateway/apps/admin_ai_context/views.py (update company_context_update view to call gRPC), services/gateway/grpc_clients/ai_client.py (use existing UpdateContextAgentBucket stub)
Gotchas: AI service gRPC stub already exists from M8. The gRPC method is async (returns 202), so don't block the HTTP response. Use try/except around gRPC call, return 500 if it fails. The AI service handles DELETE + re-chunk + re-embed internally.

ai_specs: UpdateContextAgentBucket gRPC — accepts {bucket_id, sections, free_text} → returns 202 ACCEPTED. AI service deletes all context_chunks for bucket_id, re-chunks content, embeds chunks, inserts new context_chunks rows.
existing_code: services/gateway/grpc_clients/ai_client.py: AIClient with stub methods for AI gRPC calls

userStories.title: Monitoring Backend Service — Celery Periodic Task
userStories.description: As the system, I want a periodic background task that checks service health and sends alerts when issues are detected so that admins are notified of problems.
acceptanceCriteria: Celery periodic task runs on configurable interval (admin_parameter: health_check_interval, default 60s)
Checks: gateway HTTP health, AI gRPC health, PDF gRPC health, notification consumer heartbeat, DB connection, Redis PING, broker connection, DLQ message counts
Each check stores result {service_name, status (healthy/unhealthy), last_check timestamp} in Redis with TTL
If any check fails or DLQ depth > dlq_alert_threshold, publish monitoring.alert event
Event payload: {alert_type, service_name, details, timestamp}
Task logs all checks and results
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/tech-stack.md § Monitoring Service Design — Celery periodic task, health checks, DLQ monitoring
Design: docs/03-design/component-specs.md § ServiceHealthTable — status dots + latency + last check
Testing: docs/04-test-architecture/test-matrix.md § T-11.5.01 (Integration: monitoring service detects unhealthy service)
Files: services/core/apps/monitoring/tasks.py (new), services/core/apps/monitoring/health_checks.py (new), services/core/apps/monitoring/tests/test_monitoring_service.py (new)
Gotchas: Store health check results in Redis with key prefix 'monitoring:health:{service_name}' and TTL = 2 * health_check_interval. Use Celery beat schedule to run task. DLQ depth check requires broker management API (RabbitMQ) or SDK (Azure Service Bus).

data_model: admin_parameters: health_check_interval integer (default 60), dlq_alert_threshold integer (default 10) — configurable monitoring params
test_cases: T-11.5.01 | Integration | Monitoring service detects unhealthy service | Health check fails for one service | Alert email sent to opted-in admins

userStories.title: Alert Configuration + Dispatch — Admin Opt-In & Email
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to opt in or out of monitoring alerts, and when an alert is triggered, I want to receive an email if I'm opted in so that I'm notified of system issues.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/admin/monitoring/alerts returns {user_id, is_active} for current admin user
PATCH /api/admin/monitoring/alerts accepts {is_active} and updates monitoring_alert_configs table
monitoring.alert event consumed by notification service
Notification service queries opted-in admins (is_active=true) from monitoring_alert_configs
Email sent to each opted-in admin with alert details (service, issue, timestamp)
Email subject: '[ZiqReq Alert] {service_name} - {alert_type}'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/api-design.md § Admin → Monitoring alerts endpoints
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/data-model.md § monitoring_alert_configs table — admin opt-in to alerts
Testing: No dedicated test IDs — integration covered by T-11.5.01 (alert email sent)
Files: services/gateway/apps/monitoring/views.py (add alert config endpoints), services/gateway/apps/monitoring/serializers.py, services/notification/consumers.py (add monitoring_alert_consumer), services/notification/email_templates/monitoring_alert.html (new), services/gateway/apps/monitoring/tests/test_alert_config.py (new)
Gotchas: MonitoringAlertConfig table already exists (gateway service owns it). Create config row on first GET if none exists (upsert pattern). Notification service already has email dispatch infrastructure from M12.

data_model: monitoring_alert_configs: id uuid PK, user_id uuid UNIQUE, is_active boolean (default false), created_at, updated_at — admin alert opt-in
api_endpoints: GET /api/admin/monitoring/alerts — get current admin's alert config → 200 {user_id, is_active}
PATCH /api/admin/monitoring/alerts {is_active} — update alert config → 200 {user_id, is_active}

userStories.title: Admin Panel Layout — /admin Route with 4 Tabs
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to navigate to /admin and see a 4-tab layout (AI Context, Parameters, Monitoring, Users) so that I can manage system configuration.
acceptanceCriteria: Route /admin renders AdminPanel page component
Admin role required (redirect to /landing for non-admins)
4 tabs: AI Context (Brain icon), Parameters (Settings icon), Monitoring (BarChart3 icon), Users (Users icon)
Tab navigation changes content area without page reload
Active tab highlighted with gold underline
Navbar 'Admin' link visible only to admins
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Design: docs/03-design/page-layouts.md § S4 Admin Panel — 4 tabs with icons
Design: docs/03-design/component-specs.md § Tabs — horizontal tabs with icons
Testing: docs/04-test-architecture/test-matrix.md § UI-ADMIN.01 (4 tabs render), UI-ADMIN.04 (non-admin access denied)
Files: frontend/src/pages/admin-panel.tsx (replace placeholder), frontend/src/components/layout/Navbar.tsx (add Admin link with role gate)
Gotchas: Use shadcn Tabs component (@radix-ui/react-tabs). Lucide icons: Brain, Settings, BarChart3, Users. Role check uses useAuth hook (admin role in roles array).

components: Tabs — horizontal tabs with shadcn Tabs, TabsList, TabsTrigger, TabsContent
test_cases: UI-ADMIN.01 | Unit | 4 tabs render: AI Context, Parameters, Monitoring, Users | Admin role | All tabs visible
UI-ADMIN.04 | Unit | Non-admin sees 403 or redirect | User role only | Access denied

userStories.title: AI Context + Parameters Tabs — Editors & Table
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to edit facilitator and company context, and view/edit system parameters in a table so that I can configure AI context and runtime parameters.
acceptanceCriteria: AI Context tab: 2 sections (Facilitator + Company Context) with textarea editors
Facilitator section: label, textarea (content), save button
Company Context section: label, sections editor (JSON or structured inputs), free_text textarea, save button
Save buttons call PATCH endpoints and show success/error toasts
Parameters tab: table with columns (Key, Value, Default, Description, Category)
Parameter value inline editable (click to edit, Enter to save, Escape to cancel)
Gold indicator shown for modified parameters (value != default_value)
Parameter changes saved via PATCH /api/admin/parameters/:key
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Design: docs/03-design/page-layouts.md § S4 Admin Panel — AI Context editors, parameters table
Design: docs/03-design/component-specs.md § Textarea — auto-grow variant
Testing: docs/04-test-architecture/test-matrix.md § UI-ADMIN.02 (parameters tab shows editable fields)
Files: frontend/src/features/admin/AIContextTab.tsx (new), frontend/src/features/admin/ParametersTab.tsx (new), frontend/src/api/admin.ts (new), frontend/src/pages/admin-panel.tsx (wire tabs)
Gotchas: Company context sections field is JSONB — use JSON editor or structured inputs. For MVP, use textarea with JSON.parse validation. Parameters table uses shadcn Table component. Inline editing pattern: double-click row to edit, Enter to save, Escape to cancel.

components: Textarea — auto-grow variant, used for AI context editors
ParametersTable — inline-editable table with gold modified indicator
api_endpoints: GET /api/admin/ai-context/facilitator → {id, content, updated_by, updated_at}
PATCH /api/admin/ai-context/facilitator {content} → 200
GET /api/admin/ai-context/company → {id, sections, free_text, updated_by, updated_at}
PATCH /api/admin/ai-context/company {sections, free_text} → 200
GET /api/admin/parameters → [{key, value, default_value, description, data_type, category, updated_by, updated_at}]
PATCH /api/admin/parameters/:key {value} → 200
test_cases: UI-ADMIN.02 | Unit | Parameters tab shows editable fields | Parameter data | Input fields with current values

userStories.title: Monitoring + Users Tabs — Dashboard & Search
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to view monitoring stats (KPI cards, service health, alert config) and search for users so that I can monitor system health and find user information.
acceptanceCriteria: Monitoring tab: KPI cards (Active Connections, Ideas by State, Active Users, AI Requests)
Service health table: columns (Service, Status, Last Check) with status dots
Alert configuration: chip list showing opted-in admins, add/remove buttons
Users tab: search input + user card grid
Search triggers GET /api/admin/users/search?q=query
User cards show: avatar, name, email, roles, stats (idea count, review count, contribution count)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Design: docs/03-design/page-layouts.md § S4 Admin Panel — monitoring dashboard, user search
Design: docs/03-design/component-specs.md § KPICard, UserCard, ServiceHealthTable
Testing: docs/04-test-architecture/test-matrix.md § UI-ADMIN.03 (monitoring dashboard renders stats)
Files: frontend/src/features/admin/MonitoringTab.tsx (new), frontend/src/features/admin/UsersTab.tsx (new), frontend/src/components/admin/KPICard.tsx (new), frontend/src/components/admin/ServiceHealthTable.tsx (new), frontend/src/components/admin/UserCard.tsx (new), frontend/src/api/admin.ts (add endpoints)
Gotchas: KPI cards use TanStack Query with refetchInterval for live updates. Service health status: green dot (healthy), red dot (unhealthy). User search debounced (300ms) to avoid excessive API calls.

components: KPICard — label, value, optional trend indicator
UserCard — avatar, name, email, role badges, stats
ServiceHealthTable — service, status dots, last check timestamp
api_endpoints: GET /api/admin/monitoring → {active_connections, ideas_by_state, active_users, online_users, ai_processing, system_health}
GET /api/admin/monitoring/alerts → {user_id, is_active}
PATCH /api/admin/monitoring/alerts {is_active} → 200
GET /api/admin/users/search?q=query → [{id, email, first_name, last_name, display_name, roles, idea_count, review_count, contribution_count}]
test_cases: UI-ADMIN.03 | Unit | Monitoring dashboard renders stats | Monitoring data | Charts/numbers displayed
